final_text:
I have enough to decide. Comparison:

- **DNTTIP1** — eliminated: housekeeping (K562 major, 131 DE) and constitutive (high rest DE).
- **LRBA** — strong and stim-specific, cell-type-specific, but flagged `is_activation_module: true`, so it reads as part of the activation program rather than an upstream regulator of it.
- **PGGT1B** — the only condition-specific regulator: near-inert at rest (175 DE) but 3014 DE at Stim8hr with confirmed on-target KD; not canonical, not an activation-module gene, not an essentiality artifact; cell-type-specific (K562 inert, 1 DE); and no annotated regulon in CollecTRI.

PGGT1B satisfies every criterion best.

{"gene": "PGGT1B", "hypothesis": "PGGT1B (geranylgeranyltransferase-I beta subunit) is a stimulation-gated, cell-type-specific regulator of the CD4+ T-cell activation transcriptome, likely acting upstream via prenylation-dependent signaling rather than as a transcription factor.", "evidence": ["In the frozen screen PGGT1B is classed as a condition_specific_regulator with on-target KD", "Near-inert at rest (175 DE) but 3014 DE genes at Stim8hr, i.e. a stimulation-gated effect", "Not a canonical T-cell gene, not an activation-module gene, not an essentiality artifact", "Cell-type-specific: knockdown is inert in non-immune K562 (1 DE), verdict cell-type-specific", "Not an annotated TF and has no known regulon in CollecTRI (0 targets)"], "why_novel": "It has no annotated regulon and is not a known TF, yet its knockdown reshapes thousands of genes only upon stimulation and only in T cells, pointing to an under-appreciated, likely prenylation-mediated regulatory role rather than a housekeeping or canonical T-cell function."}

hypothesis.hypothesis: PGGT1B (geranylgeranyltransferase-I beta subunit) is a stimulation-gated, cell-type-specific regulator of the CD4+ T-cell activation transcriptome, likely acting upstream via prenylation-dependent signaling rather than as a transcription factor.
evidence: In the frozen screen PGGT1B is classed as a condition_specific_regulator with on-target KD
Near-inert at rest (175 DE) but 3014 DE genes at Stim8hr, i.e. a stimulation-gated effect
Not a canonical T-cell gene, not an activation-module gene, not an essentiality artifact
Cell-type-specific: knockdown is inert in non-immune K562 (1 DE), verdict cell-type-specific
Not an annotated TF and has no known regulon in CollecTRI (0 targets)
hypothesis.why_novel: It has no annotated regulon and is not a known TF, yet its knockdown reshapes thousands of genes only upon stimulation and only in T cells, pointing to an under-appreciated, likely prenylation-mediated regulatory role rather than a housekeeping or canonical T-cell function.
